title: Register Access — Talking to the Metal
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Memory-mapped register access via volatile pointers and bit-field structs provides zero-overhead hardware control
content.proof: ARM peripherals live at fixed addresses. A struct with volatile fields overlaid at that address gives named access to individual bits. The compiler generates single load/store instructions. No function call overhead. The hardware sees the access in one cycle.
content.benchmark: Register access via volatile struct: 1 cycle. Via function call wrapper: 3-5 cycles. Via HAL library: 20-50 cycles.
content.caveat: Volatile is not a memory barrier. For DMA-coherent access, you need DMB/DSB instructions. C doesn't give you those — use inline asm or compiler builtins.
